steps.title: Identify what makes a valid proxy
steps.content: The hypothesis proposes that A. aquaticus can serve as a proxy for P. perezi in WC-NP bioaccumulation testing—meaning measurements in the invertebrate would reliably indicate what happens in the vertebrate, eliminating the need for expensive vertebrate testing.

steps.title: Test which finding breaks the proxy
steps.content:
For a proxy to work reliably, the two species must accumulate WC-NPs in comparable, predictable ways.

Choice B — The rate of WC-NP uptake in *A. aquaticus* differs from the rate of WC-NP uptake in *P. perezi* in a way that is not yet well understood by researchers. directly breaks this: if the rate of WC-NP uptake differs between the species in ways researchers don't yet understand, you cannot use A. aquaticus measurements to predict P. perezi levels.

Choices A and D actually strengthen proxy viability by showing reduced harm and consistency, while C addresses accumulation of other nanoparticles rather than the WC-NP proxy mechanism itself.

That matches Choice B — The rate of WC-NP uptake in *A. aquaticus* differs from the rate of WC-NP uptake in *P. perezi* in a way that is not yet well understood by researchers..
